title: homogyne alpina subsp. cantabrica
intro: Homogyne alpina subsp. cantabrica, also known as the Cantabrian alpine homogyne, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the genus Homogyne and the family Asteraceae. It is a perennial plant native to the mountainous regions of the Iberian Peninsula, and has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may help to alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, and promote wound healing. Herbal preparations of Homogyne alpina subsp. cantabrica include infusions, tinctures, and decoctions, which can be consumed as teas or used topically to harness its potential health benefits.
